content: Hi, I'm a PhD student in Computational Linguistics, and I'm looking for new hobbies to try in my free time.
That's a fascinating field of study. What kind of hobbies are you interested in trying?
I've been really into marathon running lately, and I've also been learning about Japanese cuisine. I even tried making sushi last weekend.
Marathon running is a great way to stay active, and Japanese cuisine is so delicious. Have you tried any new restaurants in Amsterdam where you're based?
Yes, I've been trying out a few new places. I recently discovered a great ramen shop near my apartment. And I've also been restoring a vintage car in my free time.
That sounds like a fun project. What kind of vintage car are you restoring?
It's a 1969 Ford Mustang. I've always been a fan of classic cars, and I'm excited to get it back on the road.
That's a beautiful car. Have you thought about entering it into any car shows or events?
Yes, I've been thinking about it. I've also been considering starting a YouTube channel to document my restoration process and share my knowledge with others.
That's a great idea. Sharing your passion with others can be really rewarding. Have you thought about what kind of content you'll create for your channel?
I'm thinking of doing some tutorials on mechanical keyboard building, as well as sharing my experiences with marathon running and Japanese cuisine.
That sounds like a great mix of topics. I'm sure your channel will be really popular.
